transcript.text: Good morning.
Good afternoon.
How's it going, Joao?
Nice to meet you.
Hi, Tyler.
I know that you pronounce my name in the Portuguese fashion.
I do appreciate you for that.
I was trying my best.
What is the best pronunciation for your name?
You're actually the first Joao that I've ever met.
Well, pretty common in Brazil.
I guess you've never gone to Carnival.
But, like, you know, you actually pronounced it correctly.
Thank you very much.
I really appreciate you, Tyler.
Is Tyler correct?
That's correct.
That one's pretty easy.
But, no, it's great to meet you and appreciate your time here today.
Just kind of a quick agenda for the call.

You know, what I really love to focus on is just kind of better understanding your interest in growthx, you know, what you're looking to do from a content perspective, and then help you better understand, right, how our broadcast works and what you're actually getting from our service.
I
How does that sound for today?
Sure.
I'm on the same page.
I guess if you just need like a quick one-liner, we tried land-based.
If you've heard of those folks, it didn't really make the cut.
I mean, we tried land-based.
I mean, they're good in certain aspects, but a bit about us.
My name is Joao.
As you know, I'm one of the three co-founders of Screenloop.
You can think of us as a Workday with AI.
So we're an ATS-like Workday, but we're AI first.
I'm sure that Workday have their own AI, but, you know, we raise money based on AI just like everyone else.
I guess like we're doing okay.
We're approaching four customers.
We're approaching six million ARR.
And I guess the perspective here really is that we need to further supercharge our growth.
Because like, you know, VCs have this distorted view of what success is these days.
So they keep comparing us to like stories like lovable.
Oh, they got to like, you know, a hundred million ARR in eight months.
So nothing is ever good enough anymore.
You call yourself dot AI.
And like the perspective is that one of multiple marketing.
We're looking for the best possible vendor who can help us automate content marketing.
For me, the ideal end state is that you want to be a very lean team.
Currently, we're at Seed.
We're about 30 people.
Ideally, we close our Series A.
We're currently in the middle of our Series A.
We're getting internal commitments.
And since we have internal commitments, we have budget for new software.
So ideal end state is that we probably have three to five people for the marketing core team.
And instead of hiring, let's say, you know, people who may or may not work out, like we don't want to work with agencies.
We don't want to work with freelancers.
We just want to automate it all.
But it's all being done by one or two of what I call full-stack marketers.
The future of programmers and full-stack developers, the future of marketing in the same world is full-stack marketers.
So So

If there's a tool out there who can help us automate content generation and a platform that will allow a human to actually sift through the content and publish efficiently, that's what we're here for.
That's perfect.
No, I think that's a great fit for what we do here.
And how did you come across GrowthX?
Google.
Google, just like you search like automate content marketing or something?
Yeah.
As I mentioned, we're trying to canvas tools right now.
And I just started my product discovery journey.
Part of it was Google, part of it was ChatGPT.
So I assembled a list of 20 vendors from like, you know, these sources, and then I'm just going through the list.
So I just started this, this week.
And I guess just to help you signpost, like what I envisage this to be is like a six to seven week process.
I want to take a month just taking demos because if there's one thing I know in the SaaS space, there are a lot of marketing.
I vendors.
So I just want to make sure I be exhaustive about it.
And once I get vendors, hopefully by week four, I reach out to you on email.
We inform each other if there's a shortlist.
So I don't hopefully bother you for a third call or a fourth call until we're on each other's shortlist.
If that makes sense.
I appreciate that.
Yeah, that makes perfect sense to me.
And this is actually my third week at GrowthX.
So that helps me kind of understand when to loop in our CEO, Marcel, or our VP of customer growth, Kyle.
But you'll walk away today like with a good understanding of how GrowthX is different in the space.
One clarifying question here.
I guess the timeline is connected to your next raise, correct?
You would need to raise your next round before you make your investment decision?
Not necessarily, no.
I mean, hopefully we sign a contract by week seven or eight.
The only perspective is that I feel like I need to front load the work.
A lot of the work will be made.
Just going through the demos, I think I got to spend one month on it.
And then week five, we get to a short list.
We have like a third round of calls.
And then that's when you maybe bring on the CEO.
Because like speaking as a co-founder, you will want to disappoint them with a guy.
So for what it's worth, I just want to like, yeah.
I mean, I don't know.
Maybe by week four, I'll let you know if like we feel good about it.
But in case we don't, we don't waste your time.
And for what it's worth, I wish you the best.
Like it's only your third week, but I wish you all the best.
I know how startup world is like quite stressful.
No, that means a lot, Joao.
It's definitely been like drinking from a fire hose, but a lot of fun just because the opportunity, I think, is so clear, right?
Like if you can figure out a way, which I think GrowthX has, to combine like human expertise with AI workflows that, you know, allow you to create content that's going to show.

Go up in LLM searches and really just allow you right to your point, not hire an agency that'll just do the SEO piece, but have a partner that's going to help you from strategy to content creation, even hitting publish and posting in your CMS.
So I'll explain more to you about what GravesX is about.
Before that, just so I respect your time, because I've been in these calls where they invest 40 minutes of time only at the end to find out that we can afford it.
So I prefer to start these calls with a very basic question.
What is the minimum contract size?
Because if we can't afford it, it's just better to give you back your time.
It's always good to just talk about how much it would cost and then we talk about the value.
Absolutely.
So we work in two different phases.
The first phase is an eight-week strategy sprint.
The strategy sprint would be a 20K fixed fee and then you would have the ability to fire us, right?
Or we could even fire you.
During this eight-week period, and we'd move into a 12-month growth execution, that would be $15,000 per month at the starting price.
$180,000 is within our budget.
I guess my question there is, does the $180,000 per year include the budget for the marketing itself?
Because I would imagine that depending on the content marketing, we would need to pay for certain things, like publishing costs, whatnot.
You know what I mean?
What do you guys use for your CMS today?
I have to get back to you.
I'm the data engineer guy, so I'm sorry.
I didn't feel prepared, but like, could you like rattle off some names?
I think I may remember.
Yeah, like Webflow, WordPress, you know, Adobe Experience Manager.
There's a lot of different concept management systems.
Contentful.
We use Contentful.
There you go.
There you go.
The last one.
Was the one.
No, that makes sense.
And I think, you we should definitely help you understand, like, what all you're getting from GrowthX before we go too far down the pricing rabbit hole.
But essentially, that cost is going to include everything, Joao.
So it's going to include, right, the content creation, the publishing.
You're not going to need additional tools.
Like, what GrowthX is providing you with is, it's a lot more, like, blog content, right, or thought leadership.
It's not, you know, anything that you would have to pay an additional cost to be able to publish, unless you have, like, a specific concern in mind.
Tyler, is it okay if I just ask a couple more questions?
Because, like, I think I kind of understand where you're going with this.
I mean, I've come across a bunch of vendors, like, in other spaces where, like, they call themselves, like, service as a software, some BS like that.
I mean, like, what I'm hearing is that it's.
It seems that you are also an agency, because there's like a strategy session, and then there's like an implementation phase.
It almost feels like you are an agency, but you're using AI to multiply the scope of what your agency could do.
I just want to be very clear, are we hiring an agency?
Because it sounds like we are.
I mean that in the most respectful way.
No, understood.
I think, let me just give you like a short presentation that'll help you understand like what we are.
We absolutely are doing what an agency would do, but there is an AI platform that powers our work, right?
And our founder and CEO, I mean, he basically started his career leading content marketing for, you know, IBM and HP.
At IBM, he built, right, this website called securityintelligence.com.
It drove over $50 million in pipeline for their business.
At HP, he built a newsletter with, right, a contributor network of like a thousand experts.
So...
Thank

Organic growth is basically Marcel's specialty, and he was the CMO at Service Titan, Scale AI, and then helped DeepGram basically use what we're doing here at GrowthX, these AI workflows with experts in the loop, to drive organic traffic and really systematically create a way of showing up in search.
So does that make a little bit more sense about our differentiation here?

I want to make sure you understand we're not just another agency, and I can show you the software under the hood today so you can see how it works, but does this clear up a little bit of how GrowthX is different?
You've optimized a model such that the content the AI produces is more SEO optimized?
Because when you say show up in search, we're talking SEO, we're also talking LLMs.
These days, a lot of people search you LLMs, right?
So when you say content marketing, who picks it up?
Just Google.
Totally valid question, right?
And our focus is on both sides.
As you know, the world is shifting from traditional search to AI search.
You're seeing right up to 70% of clicks disappear, but AI search visits are 4.4 times more likely to convert, right?
So we are absolutely thinking about the GEO, AEO lens.
Some of the things that we're doing here, it's not just net new content creation, it's content refreshes, right?
Because what we're seeing is the most important factors is, is the content fresh?
Has it been researched and attributed?
And so the examples that I show you, right, we're working with, you know, customers like Ramp, Webflow, Augment Code.
You'll see that what we're doing is creating content that's actually showing up in these searches.
Would you like to, would you like me to go into maybe some of the content examples and the platform itself?
Would that be helpful?
That's a good idea, but before we proceed, you have some impressive customers, but social proof-wise, how long have you been around?
Like, are you guys, like, have you guys just launched, or how many customers are using you, just trying to assess how mature the platform is?
Yeah, understood.
So GrowthX, we've been around for about a year.
We've already grown to over $7 million in ARR and raised a Series A from Madrona.
So we are definitely, you know, onto something when it comes to being able to create something that's more systematic, right, about having content that's going to drive organic traffic.
So that's, you know, been something I've been lucky to, right, join and be a part of, and one of the reasons why I'm enjoying drinking from the fire hose, right?
I hope I'm not overwhelming you.
I've been a bit of a prick, but, like, you must understand, I'm just trying to...
I'll protect my own company, right?
No, you're totally fine.

I just wanted to make sure that you got to kind of see what we're all about and not just, you know, because I'm new, right, this assumption that we're just like any other agency that's saying that they're using AI to power, right, their services.
So I think it's definitely different here and it's hard to articulate, but no, you're challenging me in a good way, for sure.
I appreciate that.
Thank you for understanding.
I mean, just so I can repeat back what you said to me, it seems what separates you from an agency using, let's say, I don't know, copy AI on their own is you built your software on your own.
So does that mean that your services are deeply integrated with your own proprietary software?
Is that it?
Because you have prop software, you're better than the other agencies?
That's it, Joao.
I would say it's the expertise, right?
I
Of who's on our team, we have experts in the loop that basically are working with our AI workflows to just make sure the content is as good as it can possibly be.
So like our chief content officer came from TechCrunch.
We've got managing directors who ran SEO for Fanatics and TripAdvisor.
One of our managing editors built the G2.com website.
So, you know, what you're getting in growthx is we're starting with outcomes, right?
We're trying to figure out strategically, like, what are the keywords that you want to go after against your competition?
Where are there maybe opportunities for us to get very strategic about the content that we're creating?

But because these workflows are going to be garbage without the right context that's informing them, we spend a lot of time up front creating these artifacts that allow us to put in context about your company, your personas.
And so this is part of our right process.
The platform is something that...
That is rapidly evolving that we'd open up to customers in the near future.
It's not something that customers are logging into today, but it is something that our team is using to deliver the services at scale.
When you say that you're experts, you're just describing your team.
Or is this like an underlying network where you have like a, I don't know, a two-sided, a three-sided marketplace?
Is this, there's you on one side, the customers on the other, and then you're actually hiring a bunch of freelancers?
I just want to make sure that.
It's the internal team that I'm referring to, right?

Okay.
All right.
I think I can understand now.
I understand now.
Absolutely.
But I think the examples will be.
Even make it more clear.
then we can sort of align on if you think this is interesting to explore further.

An instance that you wanted to automate more of your content marketing.
Any questions jumping out to you so far?

Because I'm more of a software guy.
Absolutely.

I don't know.
don't don't know.

One thing we went through, the accuracy is really important.

Much of this process we can automate with you with the right context put in up front, which is the artifacts.

I would say that that's not accurate on our side.
The human piece is really important because we have, you know, the editors in the loop that are reviewing the outputs as the content's being created.
So I don't know what the percentage would be per se, but all of the time-intensive parts that are not the human review, you know, that is absolutely being automated.
So I guess the AI generates a lot of, like, the content, but then you have humans...
It's doing like a manual review before they like post it on like our website.
Exactly, exactly.
Like what they can do here is they can interface with the content and then they can use these artifacts to, right, give very specific commands.
So if they wanted to like make the content, you know, more technical or let's say longer, right, more detailed, they could do this as they're seeing the outputs get created step-by-step.

And what are these like, I guess I'm just curious, these time markers.

This is, I think, just how long the steps took in terms of the content.
And creation.
That's what the time is referring to there.
Could I see the other work?
I guess like just maybe some feedback.
Maybe it was because I was rushing for your presentation, but for me to appreciate the scope of what you guys can do, could I just like go into like the workflows page?
I want to see like the full range of like the processes that you could do for us.
Or if you have some graphic that lists out the key aspects of content marketing that you're capable of doing.
Yeah, absolutely.
I mean, essentially what we're doing is everything that would be involved in putting a new editorial piece on your website, right?

But that's kind of the extent of the offering right now.
The vision of where we might go would be all these different use cases here.

So what does all workflows lead to?
This leads to just the pipeline, right, of all the different workflows that are happening in real time across, right, the 50 different clients that work with us today.
Oh, I see.
But rather, how about like, let me put it this way.

You're looking for like a library, right, a library of all the different workflows.
Is that essentially it?

And that begs the question, what exactly can you do right now?
Right, right.
What we can do right now is along the lines of the examples that I've shown you.
Mitch...
Editorial content.
So content on your blog, but also different parts of your website.
So anything that, you know, we're going to create, it's going to be more editorial in nature is really growthx is bread and butter today.
How about, are you already capable of doing programmatic SEO?
I guess, what did you have in mind with that use case?
Because that's something that we have done.
We typically do it when it's a fleshed out use case that we validate right internally.
I mean, there are a bunch of AI agents that would, you give them a marketing budget, and then they reallocate your marketing budget to multiple channels.
So one month, they would put 50% into Facebook, and the other month, they would put 30% into like Google Ad Network.
In this case, programmatic SEO is doing that, but for keywords.
But my question is, what is your definition of BSEO?
Understood.
I would say like a programmatic.
A PSEO example that we've done, one would be the vendor catalog for RAM.
That would be an example.
Another one would be these voice agents that we created for VAPI.

So we created all these different voice agents for VAPI, and they're able to right here, their voice AI agents across all these different use cases, whether it's managing cancellations or another one that will come up, electronic health records, and you can interact with it in the software.
So these are two examples.
You're essentially refactoring the front end.
You're just like modifying the web pages to make it more SEO friendly.
I believe so, but I would need to just verify the answer to that question.
How about the other?
So right now we only have two use cases, but in the immediate future there will be more.
Yeah, absolutely.
It's kind of something that we can do on a case-by-case basis because...
It's just a matter of creating more of these workflows.
So if you have specific ideas, like we're all ears for sure.
Could we please go back to the list?
just trying to understand like which ones would be of interest to us, Tyler.
On this slide.
Is content repurposing already available or email marketing?
Not yet.
No, it's really just the first two is what you could think about right now.
We could consider anything else.

Absolutely.
I mean, this example, if I just kind of close on this, right?

Isn't Scrunch like a tool that you yourselves can use?

isn't Scrunch a tool that allows you to, like, it's essentially Google Analytics, but for Chachi, you can be great.

I do appreciate your patience.

hope I wasn't much of a threat.

For two use cases.

Get a proper roadmap from someone higher up.

Am I being fair?

bother you to send over your calendar just with you first?

Do you have this?
Do you have that?
Before I commit to the third call with Marcel, would I be okay?

So it's...

I appreciate your patience.
I appreciate you, Joao.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call to explore GrowthX's AI-powered content marketing automation platform for Screenloop's potential use.](https://fathom.video/share/yKATQqyZHeYgmxgoW74R3qX6iXeWHjM6?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX offers AI-driven content creation with human expert oversight, focusing on SEO/AEO optimization](https://fathom.video/share/yKATQqyZHeYgmxgoW74R3qX6iXeWHjM6?tab=summary&timestamp=566.0)
  - [Pricing: $20K 8-week strategy sprint, then $15K/month for 12-month growth execution ($180K/year)](https://fathom.video/share/yKATQqyZHeYgmxgoW74R3qX6iXeWHjM6?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
  - [Current capabilities center on editorial content and some programmatic SEO, with plans to expand offerings](https://fathom.video/share/yKATQqyZHeYgmxgoW74R3qX6iXeWHjM6?tab=summary&timestamp=1230.0)
